note: Ladder rung 4: RetiredCache (arXiv 2605.06472), the first HINT-CONSUMING policy and the headline the ladder was built toward: retired-cache with-hints vs inference-only. Lifecycle retirement hints (compaction => named dead blocks) let the policy reclaim KV that recency and frequency provably cannot tell from merely old.
provenance: This file was committed with results=TBD BEFORE the rung was run, so git timestamps prove the prediction preceded the number. Adopted after a provenance failure on this exact rung (a fiction 131.9/156.4 pair was reviewed as real before any run existed; see PRIVATE.md). The prediction below is authored independently of those fiction numbers and does not anchor to them.

config._ratio_note: relabeled to cap/T; denominator = measured distinct tokens T=3,698,880 (11-session, block256); earlier 0.67 used a looser ~1.835M denominator; run config unchanged
config.corpus_reproduction_check: LRU on this exact cell = 160.5% (matches ladder-02/03); the RetiredCache numbers will share the ladder's cell.

measured_reclaim_opportunity_wiring_diag: Fair to condition on (structural wiring diagnostic, not the outcome): the retire hints emitted on this corpus name 16 retire events, 3878 unique dead blocks, 991,232 retired tokens against the 1,229,856-token capacity (~80.6% of capacity worth of provably-dead KV that recency/frequency keep resident). This is the 'idle != dead' gap the IdleTTL and GDSF rungs both hit, quantified.

prediction_registered_before_run.degeneracy_anchor_hard: Inference-only (hints off) is EXACTLY LRU by construction (empty _retired => maintain() empty => evict() is pure LRU). Hard prediction: hints-off = 160.5%, to the decimal. If it is not exactly 160.5, the graceful-degradation contract is broken and that is a bug, not a finding.
prediction_registered_before_run.with_hints_direction: With ~80% of capacity in provably-dead blocks, retirement should convert into the LARGEST single drop in the ladder so far - the first policy to collect a big fraction of LRU's ~60 points over the oracle. Registered expectation (expectation, not target): with-hints lands well below the floor, in roughly the 115-140% band. It should NOT reach ~100%, because retirement only fixes the DEAD-block decisions; it does not order LIVE-block evictions under pressure the way Belady does, so a gap to the oracle must remain. If it lands at ~100% the reclaim did more than retirement alone should - inspect for the oracle degenerating on this cell.
prediction_registered_before_run.robustness_curve_monotone: delay: as hint delay grows, dead blocks occupy capacity longer before reclamation, so benefit shrinks monotonically from the with-hints number toward 160.5. drop: as drop probability p grows, a fraction of dead blocks are never reclaimed, so percent-of-oracle interpolates monotonically from with-hints (p=0) to ~160.5 (p=1). Both curves must be monotone and anchored at both ends; a non-monotone curve indicates a delivery bug, not a robustness finding.
prediction_registered_before_run.falsifiable_edges: Three ways this prediction dies: (1) hints-off != 160.5 (contract broken); (2) with-hints NOT substantially below the floor (the 991k-token reclaim opportunity fails to convert - would need a mechanism, e.g. the reclaimed blocks were never the ones under pressure); (3) either robustness curve non-monotone (bug).

RetiredCache_inference_only.note: hints off == LRU 160.5 EXACTLY. Degeneracy anchor CONFIRMED to the decimal; the graceful-degradation contract holds.

robustness_curve_high_pressure.note: Monotone and anchored as registered: drop p=1.0 hits 160.5 exactly (== off), drop p=0.5 interpolates (160.2), delay drifts toward the floor (159.4). The whole band is ~1.2 points wide because the effect at this cell is ~1.2 points; the curve is a confirmation of the delivery mechanism, not a large robustness story.

prediction_outcome: REFUTED at the registered cell, reported as such. (1) Degeneracy anchor CONFIRMED (hints off = 160.5 exactly). (2) Robustness monotonicity CONFIRMED. (3) The headline with-hints prediction (115-140%) is WRONG: with-hints is 159.3%, only 1.2 points below the floor. The ~991k-token reclaim opportunity almost entirely failed to convert AT THIS PRESSURE. This is falsifiable-edge (2) firing: the reclaimed blocks were freed successfully but were not the binding constraint. Note also that RetiredCache-with-hints (159.3) is still BEATEN by signal-blind WA-LRU-tuned (155.4) at this cell - the lifecycle hint loses to better live-block reuse ordering.

mechanism_data_confirmed.two_wrong_theories_disclosed: I theorized the mechanism twice before measuring, and was wrong both times - recorded because the discipline is to verify, not assert. Wrong theory A: 'retirement is redundant with recency because dead blocks are the OLDEST and LRU evicts oldest-first.' Measurement refuted it: retired blocks have mean recency-rank 0.130 (1.0=oldest), i.e. they are among the FRESHEST resident blocks (compaction retires blocks the model just used), so LRU actively PROTECTS them and retirement is NOT redundant with recency. Wrong theory B (the pre-registered guess): 'never the ones under pressure' - also not quite right; they ARE protected-by-recency blocks that get reclaimed.
mechanism_data_confirmed.what_actually_happens: Measured on the registered cell: RetiredCache makes 3537 proactive maintain() reclamations of fresh-dead blocks, and evict() chooses a retired victim ZERO times (maintain reclaims them all before pressure forces an eviction). Yet capacity misses fall only 18892 -> 18746 (37,376 tokens, 0.77%), and evict() falls back to LRU 25,056 times. The freed capacity is immediately re-consumed by the relentless working-set churn under high pressure: freeing a fresh-dead block buys a short window before the same churn evicts whatever took its place. The dead blocks were reclaimed but were not the binding constraint. The 60-point room is in the ORDERING of the 25,056 live-block evictions (where Belady wins and LRU loses), which retirement does not touch. Consistent with the WA-LRU rung: the room lives in live-block reuse ordering, not dead-block occupancy.

pressure_dependence_posthoc_but_absolute.status: POST-HOC analysis (not in the registered prediction), reported at full volume with ABSOLUTE miss counts so it cannot be a percent-of-oracle denominator artifact.
pressure_dependence_posthoc_but_absolute.finding: Retirement's benefit is strongly pressure-dependent. Avoidable capacity misses saved by hints, by pressure ratio (capacity / ~1.835M working set): ratio 0.67 -> 146 saved (0.8%); ratio 0.82 -> 335 (2.8%); ratio 0.98 -> 1,120 (23.6%). The registered high-pressure cell (0.67) is near the WEAK end for this policy. As pressure eases toward balance (ratio ~1.0) the cache is near-critical - it holds most of the working set, so dead blocks occupying capacity are exactly what tips it into avoidable misses, and reclaiming them recovers ~24% of them. At very low pressure (the 1.5-ratio control) LRU is already near-optimal (100.5%), so there is no room. Inverted-U: retirement's value peaks at MIDDLING pressure, and the one cell I registered happens to sit where churn hides it.
pressure_dependence_posthoc_but_absolute.reframe: My prediction was refuted at the tested cell, but the DIRECTIONAL intuition behind it (991k dead tokens should convert into real savings) was correct - just at a pressure I did not run. The 991k opportunity does convert; whether it shows up depends on whether dead-block occupancy is the binding constraint, which it is only near balanced pressure.

new_prediction_registered_for_the_pressu: The full pressure sweep (EVAL_PLAN grid, ratios {1.5,1.0,0.6,0.3}) should trace an inverted-U for the retirement benefit: ~0 at 1.5 (LRU already optimal), a peak near ratio ~1.0 (dead-block occupancy is the binding constraint), decaying toward 0.3-0.6 (churn absorbs reclamation). Reported as absolute avoidable-miss reduction, not percent-of-oracle, because the ratio is denominator-unstable as pressure eases. Registered before the sweep is run.
thesis_disposition: The headline pairing (retired-cache with-hints vs inference-only) was called 'the thesis meeting its judge.' The judge's verdict is nuanced, not a clean win or loss: at the high-pressure cell the retirement hint barely helps (1.2 points) and loses to signal-blind WA-LRU, because the room there is live-block ordering; but the same hint recovers ~24% of avoidable misses at balanced pressure. The hint interface has real, measured value, and that value is pressure-dependent - which is a more defensible and more interesting result than either extreme, and it makes the pressure sweep (not a single cell) the honest unit of the lifecycle claim. The remaining rungs (faithful Continuum, the economic policy) inherit this: report them as curves over pressure, not single numbers.
